topics.title: Securing Next.js Server Actions: Rate Limiting, CSRF, and next-safe-action
topics.whyTrending: User-requested + hot after CVE-2025-55182 and the React/Next.js RCE disclosures — server actions are public HTTP endpoints and most tutorials skip abuse prevention. Covers @upstash/ratelimit, Next.js built-in Origin header CSRF checks, and formalizing the middleware pattern with next-safe-action.

topics.title: From Mock Session to Production Auth: NextAuth.js v5 vs Clerk (Plus Role-Based Authorization)
topics.whyTrending: User-requested. Replacing a mock session system with a real auth provider is the #1 gap in server-actions tutorials; NextAuth v5 (Auth.js) vs Clerk is a constantly debated choice, and RBAC (e.g. admins can delete any task) is rarely shown end-to-end.

topics.title: useOptimistic in React 19: Instant UI with Server Actions and Automatic Rollback
topics.whyTrending: User-requested. React 19 made useOptimistic stable; combined with server actions it delivers instant UI feedback without API routes, and the rollback-on-error behavior is widely misunderstood.

topics.title: CVE-2025-55182 Post-Mortem: What the Next.js RCE Teaches Us About RSC Security
topics.whyTrending: A server was compromised 24h after disclosure (front page of HN); attackers targeted crypto credentials with rootkit techniques. Deserializing untrusted client input into module lookups is a lesson worth a deep dive.

topics.title: Next.js 16 'use cache' Directive: Fine-Grained Caching Without the Footguns
topics.whyTrending: Next.js 16 replaced the confusing implicit caching model with the explicit use cache directive — everyone migrating from 14/15 needs a mental-model reset.

topics.title: Partial Prerendering in Production: Static Speed with Dynamic Freshness
topics.whyTrending: PPR is stabilizing in Next.js 16 and changes how you think about static vs dynamic; Suspense boundaries now define independent streaming sections.

topics.title: React Compiler Is Here: Stop Writing useMemo and useCallback
topics.whyTrending: React 19's compiler cuts unnecessary re-renders by 25-40%; 'use compiler before manual memo' is now official Vercel guidance and invalidates years of tutorial advice.

topics.title: The Backend Is Now a Folder: Server-First Architecture in Next.js
topics.whyTrending: With stable Server Actions and Functions, many teams ship apps where the entire backend lives inside the frontend repo — an architecture shift worth an opinionated take on when it works and when it bites.

topics.title: Type-Safe Server Actions Showdown: next-safe-action vs zsa vs Rolling Your Own
topics.whyTrending: Two competing libraries (next-safe-action, zsa) formalize validation + middleware for server actions; a comparison with a hand-rolled baseline has clear SEO potential.

topics.title: Streaming AI Responses in Next.js with the Vercel AI SDK
topics.whyTrending: First-class AI integration is one of the defining 2026 Next.js trends; streaming UI with RSC + AI SDK is highly searched and very demo-able.
